text: October, nineteen ninety-seven.
In four trading days, the Hang Seng Index loses twenty-three percent of its value.

props.label: Hang Seng Index: down 23% in four trading days

text: Overnight interest rates jump from eight percent to twenty-three. At one point, two hundred and eighty.
And while everyone else is panicking, somebody on that trading floor is making money. Hi. I'm Jordan Bull.

props.title: Jordan Bull
props.subtitle: Your broker for the next twelve minutes

text: I'm going to show you the only product that turns panic into a paycheck. Options.
This is the Hang Seng Index Option. Listed on the Hong Kong exchange since March, nineteen ninety-three. The flagship index derivative of Asia.
Every point of the index is worth fifty Hong Kong dollars. European exercise. Cash settled. No shares change hands, just money.

items.title: Cash settled
items.desc: no shares change hands, just money

text: But I'm not going to lecture you. I'm going to let you live it. We built a trading desk you can play.

visual.description: Local guest title screen: 'Central Trader', Chapter 1 1997 banner, CTA 'Start the Simulator'. Static hold for the whole line.

text: Real accounts. Real progress tracking. Four days from rookie to closer.

visual.description: LIVE-SITE EXCEPTION: cut to the deployed site's sign-in screen (accounts exist there; type NOTHING), then cut back to the local guest build, start the simulator, open the Account menu and go to the progress Dashboard. Mid-scene page.goto kills in-page audio: recorder must host gate audio outside the recorded page for this clip (see RECORDING.md).
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Account menu', exact: true }).click();

text: Day one starts now. Let's sell something.

visual.description: Progress dashboard with the four day cards (all 'Not started'). Cursor settles on the Day 1 card, then clicks its Start button on 'Day one starts now', landing on the Day 1 morning meeting as the clip ends.

text: Meet Martin. Twenty years on the desk. His first rule: read the client before you touch the product.

visual.description: Day 1 morning meeting: MENTOR MARTIN avatar card ('Welcome aboard, rookie.') plus role/goal cards. Hold.

text: Two basic moves. A call is the right to buy at a fixed price. A put is the right to sell.

visual.description: Click through 'Start Morning Meeting' then 'Continue: Call and Put' to the CALL / PUT lesson; bullish and bearish cards on screen for the call/put line.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue: Call and Put/i }).click();

text: Neither right is free. You pay a premium. Think of a movie ticket. Buy it, you're in. Movie's terrible? No refunds.

visual.description: Continue to the Premium lesson: 'Pay Upfront to Buy a Future Choice' with the movie-ticket sentence; late small scroll reveals the 186-point example row.

text: Here's the call's payoff. Flat, flat, flat, then up. Below the strike you lose only the ticket price. Above it, you participate.

visual.description: Fast montage through Vanilla Rules / Update Handbook / Meet the First Client to the Product Selection desk, then the cursor parks on the Vanilla Call card whose mini payoff diagram is the flat-flat-up hockey stick.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue: Vanilla Option Rules/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Meet the First Client/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Go to Product Selection/i }).click();

text: The put is the mirror. It pays when the market falls. Insurance, basically.

visual.description: Cursor moves to the Vanilla Put card: its mini payoff diagram is the mirror (pays when the market falls). Hold there.

text: First client. Miz Li. She's bullish on the index, but she cannot stomach a big loss.

visual.description: Top-bar back arrow returns to the client profile (NOTE: on screen the Day 1 client is Ms. Li while narration says Mr. Wang; script-level mismatch, accepted). Profile rows: bullish, moderate risk, capped-loss goal.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Go back', exact: true }).click();

text: Four shelves. Vanilla call. Vanilla put. Buy the index outright. And a locked barrier product, we'll earn that one later.

visual.description: Forward again to the Product Selection desk: four shelves (Vanilla Call, Vanilla Put, Buy the Index Directly, locked Barrier Option). Scroll to show all four; clicking the locked barrier card pops Martin's 'not unlocked on day one' notice on 'we'll earn that one later'.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Go to Product Selection/i }).click();

text: Bullish view plus capped downside equals vanilla call. Click.

visual.description: On 'Click.', click the Vanilla Call Option card: 'Selected' badge plus green suitability feedback appear.

text: Before he signs, disclosure. If the option expires worthless, the entire premium is gone. We say it out loud. Compliance is not decoration.

visual.description: Confirm Product opens Risk Disclosure. Tick the three correct disclosures one by one (premium can vanish, max loss = premium, no profit guarantee); the misleading fourth stays unticked.

text: Now the market runs. Twenty-one five. Twenty-two one. Twenty-two four at expiry. The call lands in the money.

visual.description: Confirm Disclosure starts the Day 1 market run: 11-point chart animates up to 22,400 (about 5.2s at 520ms a tick), gold 'View Report' appears when it settles. Audio-gated dwell covers the run.

text: But look at the net line. Payoff minus premium. Direction right is not enough, you have to out-earn the ticket price. Remember that number.

visual.description: View Report: End-of-Day report with Payoff 400 pts, Premium 186 pts, Client P&L +214 pts (the 'net line'), grade badges. Small scroll near the end.

text: And in the real contract, every point is fifty Hong Kong dollars. Four hundred points of payoff is twenty thousand dollars. Per contract.

props.label: 400 points of payoff x HK$50 per point, per contract

text: Day two. New client, Mister Wang. And today he wants a price. Not a feeling. A number.

visual.description: Day 2 morning meeting (pricing desk intro panel). Reached via dashboard 'Start' on the Day 2 card during pre-roll.

text: Here's the secret nobody tells retail: an option price is not guessed. It is built. Branch by branch.
Start at today. Index at twenty-one thousand five hundred.

visual.description: Through 'Start the Pricing Lesson' (anchor lesson flashes by) into 'Continue: Binomial Tree Paths': path diagram with the Today node at 21,500 on top; 'Today' accordion row open by default.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Start the Pricing Lesson/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue: Binomial Tree Paths/i }).click();

text: One step ahead, two futures. Up two point six five percent, or down two point five eight. That spread comes from volatility, sixteen percent a year.

visual.description: Expand the 'Step 1 Up' accordion row (22,069 / +2.65%), then 'Step 1 Down' (20,946 / -2.58%) while the up/down node cards sit beside them.

text: The up factor is e to the sigma root delta-t. The down factor is just one over it.
And the chance of going up, the risk-neutral probability, is e to the r delta-t minus d, over u minus d. That's the whole machine.
Three steps to expiry. Eight paths. Four final prices, from nineteen nine to twenty-three two.

visual.description: Expand 'Step 2 Recombine' then 'Step 3 Expiry': the four terminal prices 23,253 / 22,069 / 20,946 / 19,879 are highlighted in the path diagram.

text: At expiry the payoff is simple. Final price minus strike of twenty-two thousand, or zero. Whichever is bigger.

visual.description: Continue to 'Payoff and Backward Induction': big formula card Payoff = max(final price - strike, 0); scroll into the full tree visual where each terminal node carries its max(S-K,0) formula.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue: Payoff and Backward Induction/i }).click();

text: Then we walk backwards. Each node is the discounted, probability-weighted average of its two children. Fold the future into the present.

visual.description: Slow scroll down the binomial tree visual, walking from the terminal payoff nodes back along the linked branches (backward induction).

text: And the tree hands us the answer. One hundred eighty-six points. That is the theoretical price of this call.

visual.description: Scroll lands on the three summary cards; cursor rests on 'Today's Price': the model gives today's theoretical price, 186 points.

text: Now the sales part. Theory says one eighty-six. The desk needs margin. Quote between plus four and plus thirty-four over theoretical and the client fills.

visual.description: Sales montage: Update Handbook, Meet the Client (Mr. Wang), Product Confirmation, Confirm Product, data desk, 'Parameters Noted, Go to the Pricing Desk'. On the pricing terminal (param strip shows Simplified Theoretical Price 186 pts) type the five parameters into the calculator: 21500 / 22000 / 2 / 16 / 0.08; sidebar shows Vanilla Call theoretical price 185.94.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Go to Product Confirmation/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Parameters Noted, Go to the Pricing Desk/i }).click();

text: Quote fat and he walks to the bank across the street. Quote thin and you ate the risk for free.

visual.description: Quote box theatre: type a fat 290 ('he walks'), hold, then a thin 150 ('you ate the risk for free').

text: Two hundred ten. Filled. That spread between one eighty-six and two ten? That's the desk's lunch.

visual.description: Type 210, 'Continue to Risk Disclosure', tick the four correct items fast, 'Confirm Risk Disclosure': client response card shows quote 210 vs theoretical 186, 'Trade Accepted' for the desk's-lunch line.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue to Risk Disclosure/i }).click();

text: Market drifts to twenty-two one. Small payoff, smaller than the premium. The client learns. So do you.

visual.description: 'View Market Settlement': Day 2 path animates 21,500 to 22,140 (4 points, about 1.6s), then 'View Report' opens the Day 2 report: payoff 140 smaller than the 210 premium.

text: Day three. Miz Chen. Sharp. She wants the same upside protection, but she calls our vanilla price expensive.

visual.description: Ms. Chen's client profile and dialogue ('that vanilla call is a bit pricey'). Dwell, then walk to Product Selection, pick the Down-and-Out Call (Selected badge), and continue to the data desk as the line ends.
preRoll: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Start the Barrier Lesson/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue: What Is Knock-Out/i }).click();
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Go to Product Selection/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Go to the Data Desk for Parameters/i }).click();

text: So we go off the shelf. Over the counter. Welcome to structured products, Hong Kong's favorite aisle.
This is a knock-out barrier option. Same call, same strike, one extra clause. If the index ever touches twenty-one thousand, the contract dies. Instantly.

props.title: Knock-Out Barrier Option
props.subtitle: Same call, same strike, one extra clause: touch 21,000 and it dies

text: Price the vanilla on a four-step tree: one thousand one hundred twelve points. Price the barrier: nine hundred thirty-four.
Same view, one hundred seventy-eight points cheaper. Why? Because she sold back the crash scenarios. The paths that touch the barrier pay nothing, so she doesn't pay for them.

props.label: points cheaper: the crash scenarios are sold back

text: Our quote band scales the same way. Fair up to about one point one eight times theoretical. Reject above one point four.

visual.description: 'Parameters Noted, Continue to Pricing': vanilla 1,112 vs down-and-out 934 example-premium cards on top. Type quote 1050 (inside the 1.183x fair band), run the four disclosure ticks, 'Confirm Risk Disclosure' lands on the accepted barrier quote receipt.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Parameters Noted, Continue to Pricing/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Continue to Risk Disclosure/i }).click();

text: Now watch the market. Up to twenty-one eight. Then down. Twenty-one four. Twenty thousand nine fifty.

visual.description: Hold the 'Trade Accepted' receipt, then 'View Market Settlement' at about 3.6s: the 6-point path animates 21,500 / 21,820 / 21,460 / 20,950 (red knock-out dot) / 21,680 / 22,400 at 520ms a tick; the status pill flips to 'Barrier Touched / Product Knocked Out' near the 20,950 beat.

text: Touch. Twenty-one thousand breached. Knocked out. Position terminated.

visual.description: Knocked-out state holds: red status pill and 'Knock-Out Result: price point #4 touched the barrier'. Small scroll brings the settlement cards in.

text: And then, of course, the market rallies to twenty-two four. She watches the move she predicted happen, with no position.

visual.description: Path already finished at 22,400 with the position dead: Final Price 22,400 card next to negative Client Net P&L. Tiny settle scroll, then hold.

text: That is the barrier trade-off in one chart. The discount is real, and so is the tripwire. A good desk sells both sentences.

props.text: The discount is real. So is the tripwire.

text: In our model the knock-out checks at discrete fixings. In the real OTC market, the term sheet defines exactly when the barrier observes. Read it.

items.title: Real OTC
items.desc: the term sheet defines when the barrier observes

text: Day four. Martin steps back. Three clients, live quotes, no hints.

visual.description: Day 4 graduation briefing ('No new products: today tests your pricing judgment', three principles, 3-clients note). Near the end click 'Start Meeting Clients' so Mr. Zhang arrives as C5-2 begins. Pre-roll seeds days 1-3 as completed in localStorage so the closing dashboard shows real tracking.
preRoll: await page.evaluate(() => { localStorage.clear(); const t = new Date().toISOString(); localStorage.setItem('ct_progress_v1', JSON.stringify({ day1: { grade: 'A', score: 95, completedAt: t }, day2: { grade: 'A', score: 95, completedAt: t }, day3: { grade: 'A', score: 95, completedAt: t }, day4: null })); });

text: Mister Zhang. Vanilla call, index at twenty-four thousand, strike twenty-four five, vol eighteen. The tree says two ninety-seven.

visual.description: Mr. Zhang profile (client 1/3), then 'I've Read the Needs, Go Quote' to the pricing panel: param card S0 24,000 / K 24,500 / sigma 18 / T 0.08; type the five params into the calculator so 'Vanilla Call theoretical price: 297.06' appears right on 'the tree says two ninety-seven'.

text: Quote inside the band. Filled.

visual.description: Type quote 320 (inside the +4/+34 band over 297) and Submit Quote: 'Trade Accepted', 'Fair price, done.'

text: Miz Li. Barrier at twenty-three thousand, vol twenty-eight. Nine eighty-one.

visual.description: Next Client: Ms. Li profile (barrier 23,000 in her param card), 'Go Quote', then rapid-fill the six barrier-calculator params 24000 / 24500 / 23000 / 2 / 28 / 0.25 so the down-and-out price 980.77 lands on 'nine eighty-one'.

text: Mister He. Deeper barrier, vol thirty-two. Eleven eighty-four. Three products, three risk profiles, one machine.

visual.description: Reveal the 980.77 price card, quote 1100, Submit, Next Client: Mr. He (judge client). 'Go Judge the Product', pick the Down-and-Out Call, 'Confirm Product, Go to Quoting', then fill his six params 25000 / 25500 / 23500 / 2 / 32 / 0.25 (1,184.20 appears at the tail). Densest scene of the video.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Go Judge the Product/i }).click();
await page.getByRole('button', { name: /Confirm Product, Go to Quoting/i }).click();

text: The desk grades every quote. Suitability, pricing, disclosure. That's the whole job in three columns.

visual.description: Quote 1350 for Mr. He, Submit, then 'View Graduation Scorecard': Filled 3/3, Product Judgment 3/3, per-client grades, overall A.

text: And everything you just watched is saved to your account. Four days, tracked.

visual.description: 'Complete Graduation' (records Day 4), open the Account menu (Guest Trader / Local progress) and click Dashboard: all four day cards show Completed with grades, progress bar 100%.
steps: await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Account menu', exact: true }).click();

text: So here's my pitch. Don't just read about derivatives. Walk the desk. Price the tree. Get knocked out once, it's educational.
Our booth is live right now. Scan, sign up, and make your first quote before the next lecture.
Educational simulator, not investment advice. Options involve risk, premiums can go to zero. Data: HKEX contract specifications. Built by our group, FIN 7870.

items.title: Risk
items.desc: options involve risk, premiums can go to zero

text: Central Trader. See you on the desk.
